note: Readiness-authored acceptance bar + independent-reproduction record. The board imports these. A row reaches 'verified' ONLY after claude-yentl personally re-derives the proof. 'accepted' is Owner-only.

P0-001.acceptance_criteria: Prod yentl.it /api/document-ingest returns 200 with extracted text for the validation PDF (no Next error page); prod home + key routes 200 and render; post-deploy battery (trust/a11y/ingestion/analysis) green; P0-002 env CRITICALs verified BEFORE deploy; the deployed commit set is coherent (not a blind 246-file dump).
P0-001.reproduction_plan: After Operator deploys: I re-curl prod document-ingest + youtube-ingest, re-run the deploy battery myself, and load prod in agent-browser. Owner turns the deploy key.

P0-002.acceptance_criteria: Live AI Gateway resolves the configured model slug (anthropic/claude-opus-4.7 or corrected) — a real call returns 200, not a gateway 404/400; prod paid route (e.g. /api/extract-claims) returns 401 when YENTL_REQUIRE_AUTH=1 and no auth; prod /api/corpus-sample returns 404 (validation-demo OFF).
P0-002.reproduction_plan: I probe the live gateway for the slug and curl the prod auth + corpus-sample behavior myself.

P0-003.acceptance_criteria: Prod /api/youtube-ingest returns real transcript_segments (>0) for a known public video — not {code:PRIVATE}. Works LOCALLY today (241 segments); must work from Vercel IPs.
proof_required: prod youtube-ingest real segments for a public video id
P0-003.reproduction_plan: I curl prod youtube-ingest for the TED fixture video and confirm >0 segments.

P0-004.acceptance_criteria: A redirect/DNS-rebind to a private IP is blocked at fetch time (not just pre-check) in media-ingest + article-ingest; transcribe-batch {url} branch calls assertSafeUrl; legitimate public fetches still succeed; tests cover the rebind/redirect case.
P0-004.reproduction_plan: I re-run the new SSRF tests and attempt a redirect-to-127.0.0.1 against each route myself.

P0-005.acceptance_criteria: Security events persist to a real sink (not a no-op); a simulated model-slug error does NOT silently 503-refuse every claim invisibly — the failure is observable/alerting, and the gate fail-mode is a deliberate, documented choice.
proof_required: model-error path emits an observable signal
P0-005.reproduction_plan: I force a model error locally and confirm the event is recorded + the gate behavior is the documented one.

P1-001.acceptance_criteria: All 5 hardening headers present on every response (HSTS 63072000+includeSubDomains+preload, nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy disabling camera/geo/payment but PRESERVING microphone, CSP-Report-Only default-src 'self' + Deepgram/Gateway allowlist); tsc+lint+test green; verified via live curl.
P1-001.reproduction_plan: Re-verify on prod after P0-001 deploy; then Owner accepts. Promote CSP to enforce after report window is clean.

P1-002.acceptance_criteria: A single verdict vocabulary (lib/client/verdict-theme.ts) renders across watch-view, claim-row, claim-detail, chips, markers, TV, mobile — no component shows raw taxonomy labels that contradict it; VerdictChip text/!bg contrast >= 4.5:1 (WCAG AA); verdicts carry icon+text+color (not hue-only); markers carry a severity-scaled icon + a plain word (e.g. 'Inflammatory'); all existing tests stay green; before/after screenshots captured.
P1-002.reproduction_plan: I re-run the suite, grep for the removed hardcoded maps, and measure rendered contrast in agent-browser myself before marking verified. (Grok builds in a worktree; I do NOT rubber-stamp.)

P1-003.acceptance_criteria: Diarization is ON only behind an explicit, granular consent that the user actively grants; a voiceprint-deletion path exists and works; a recorded legal sign-off (DPIA/TIA update) authorizes biometric processing; default remains OFF. (Owner-gated — autonomy rule 11.)
P1-003.reproduction_plan: I verify diarize stays false without consent and only flips with it; I confirm the deletion path and the recorded sign-off.

P1-004.acceptance_criteria: Real multi-speaker accuracy is measured on the ACTUAL shipping pipeline (post-P1-003), on at least one real 30-45min multi-speaker debate with human-labeled ground truth; WER + speaker-attribution numbers recorded with the config they were measured under; no public 'robust multi-speaker' claim exceeds the measured evidence.
P1-004.reproduction_plan: I re-score at least one sample myself and confirm the public copy matches the measured number.

P1-005.acceptance_criteria: A real public multi-speaker audio URL flows media-ingest -> real Deepgram -> a real transcript (utterances+speakers), NOT a fixture/replay; artifact saved.
proof_required: real Deepgram transcript artifact from a real URL
P1-005.reproduction_plan: I run it myself end-to-end and save the transcript; confirm it is not the validation fixture path.

P1-006.acceptance_criteria: An Owner-approved design direction exists; each primary surface (Watch, TV, mobile) presents ONE dominant verdict legible at a glance and from a distance; signal language is consistent and consumer-plain (no Meta-Read/Pulse/Counter-read jargon surfaced raw); screenshots match the approved direction.
P1-006.reproduction_plan: Start with P1-002. I draft the redesign direction for Owner approval, then verify built screens against the approved frames.

P2-001.acceptance_criteria: Every proof script/JSON is labeled REAL or SYNTHETIC; the ingestion-deploy proof logic is fixed so a live 5xx fails the check (cannot read ok:true with a 500 hidden in deploy_blockers).
proof_required: induced prod 5xx makes the proof fail
P2-001.reproduction_plan: I re-read the proof logic and confirm a 500 now fails it.

P2-002.acceptance_criteria: Every screen + each meaningful state from docs/audit/2026-06-19/03-frontend-ux.md inventory is captured (desktop + ~390 mobile) into the screenshots folder.
proof_required: screenshot set covering the inventory checklist
P2-002.reproduction_plan: I spot-check the captured set against the inventory.
